Pre-built health snapshot block with start/end markers.
File name constant for the custom .ignore file used by ArchSpine's search mechanism to exclude noisy directories.
File name constant for the .spineignore file that controls semantic scanning of the repository.
Array of lines for the .ignore file, excluding generated and low-signal paths from agent workspace search.
Complete newline-separated string content for the .ignore file built from SEARCH_IGNORE_LINES.
Comment marker line indicating the start of the ArchSpine-recommended section in .spineignore.
Comment marker line indicating the end of the ArchSpine-recommended section in .spineignore.
Array of recommended lines for .spineignore including block markers and exclusion patterns for generated, local, and high-noise files.
Comment marker line indicating the start of the ArchSpine-managed section in .gitignore.
Comment marker line indicating the end of the ArchSpine-managed section in .gitignore.
Comment marker line indicating the start of the ArchSpine-managed section in .gitattributes.
Comment marker line indicating the end of the ArchSpine-managed section in .gitattributes.
Array of .gitignore lines for ArchSpine-managed files (cache.db, lock file, etc.), including the .mcp.json entry.
Re-exports all symbols from the agent instructions templates module, providing template definitions for agent instruction formatting and generation.
Re-exports all symbols from the agent instructions synchronization module, providing logic to synchronize instruction sets across environments.
Prints a large ASCII art banner with the ArchSpine logo and version. Called once at the start of interactive or verbose CLI sessions.
Prints a compact one-line colored banner showing 'ArchSpine' in brand colors. Used for quick command invocations where a full banner is excessive.
Displays a prompt message and waits for the user to type a response ('y' or 'n'), returning true for yes and false for no. Uses the prompts library for input.
Displays a prompt message and listens for a single keypress ('y' or 'n') in raw mode, returning true for yes and false for no, with colored output and Ctrl+C handling.
Class exposing static methods for atomic filesystem operations. Consumed by infrastructure modules that need safe write or copy capabilities.
Writes content to a file atomically by writing to a temporary file and then renaming it. Used to ensure data integrity during scans, syncs, and artifact generation.

Discriminated union type representing the outcome of a git hook installation: success with status ('installed', 'updated', 'appended', 'standardized') or skip reason ('skipped-no-git-root') with a message.
Installs or updates the ArchSpine pre-commit hook script in `.git/hooks/pre-commit`. Returns an InstallGitHookResult indicating whether the hook was installed, updated, or skipped.
Removes the ArchSpine pre-commit hook from `.git/hooks/pre-commit` if it exists. Returns a boolean indicating success.
Class that acquires and releases file-based locks on the .spine directory. Used by any ArchSpine component that needs exclusive access to the runtime environment.
Parses a JSON string into a RuntimeLockPayload object. Enables external code to inspect the content of an existing lock file.
Serializes a RuntimeLockPayload object into a JSON string. Used to write lock files with the correct payload structure.
Interface defining the shape of a lock payload (token and timestamp). Implemented by lock files and used by consumers creating or validating locks.

Normalizes a raw file system path string to a canonical repo-relative path with forward slashes. Input backslashes are replaced, and leading './' or '/' are removed. Used by all modules that interact with the database and file system indices.

nodes.role: Primary orchestration service for the ArchSpine 'check' pipeline — coordinates scanning, AST extraction, validation, and cleanup tasks in a single workflow. Used by CLI commands and programmatic consumers to run governance checks on a repository.
responsibilities: Expose `runCheck` function for simple CLI/one-shot usage with minimal configuration (rootDir, optional llmClient, optional scanPolicy).
Expose `CheckService` class for programmatic orchestration with full options via `CheckServiceOptions` (rootDir, llmClient, scanPolicy, runtimeIO).

Orchestrate the full check workflow: start a runtime session, resolve scan policy, create task context (with checkpoint for resumability), run ScanAndCleanupTask, ASTExtractionTask, and ValidationTask sequentially via TaskRunner.
Report stage-level metrics (bytes processed, durations) to the runtime IO for observability.
Handle errors by converting them to ArchSpineError via toArchSpineError and log error details.
Orchestrate a sequence of fix tasks (FixTask, ScanAndCleanupTask, ASTExtractionTask, ValidationTask) in a defined pipeline.
Manage retry logic with up to MAX_FIX_RETRIES (2) fix attempts per run.
Provide both a standalone function (runFix) and a class-based service (FixService) to initiate fix runs.
Integrate with runtime session via runWithRuntimeSession to provide structured logging and error handling.
Coordinate validation and re-check after fixes to measure remaining violations.
Track fix run metrics via FixRunSummary including initial and remaining violations.
Accept root directory, project configuration, and an optional runtime service context.
Initialize a Scanner instance from the engine layer and gather all tracked files under the root directory using the configured scan policy.
Invoke the AST-level discoverLanguages function to detect programming languages from the collected file paths and optional additional binary paths.
Open or create a Manifest via the infra layer and persist the resulting LanguageSnapshot (metadata including detected languages and file counts).
Return the LanguageSnapshot for the caller (e.g., CLI bootstrap) to use or display.
Resolve the appropriate config/secrets store and label for LLM configuration targeting either global or project scope.
Persist a single LLM setting (provider, model, base URL, or API key) into the target configuration or secrets store.
Clear a persisted LLM API key from the target secrets store.
Save a complete LLM setup (provider, model, and API key) in one operation to the target stores.
Build a status view model that aggregates LLM settings from both global and project scopes for user-facing display.
Ensure the `.spine` directory exists in the target root directory before publishing.
Validate that the snapshot index is present and not locked (i.e., no active compressed snapshot being written).
Warn when the current artifact strategy is set to 'local' to avoid unintentional publishing of local-only artifacts.
Define the `ResolvedExecutionProfile` interface that encapsulates the generation strategy and its source of truth.
Define the `RuntimeCommand` union type representing commands supported by the runtime (sync, check, fix).
Export `resolveExecutionProfileFromSettings` function that transforms an external `ResolvedLLMSettings` object into a `ResolvedExecutionProfile` for downstream runtime consumption.
Initialize and expose Config, Secrets, and LLM client (ResolvedLLMSettings) based on runtime settings
Resolve and instantiate the view layer (resolveViewLayer, resolveEnabledViews) from `./view/index.js`
Create SyncService, CheckService, and FixService instances with properly built options (CheckServiceOptions, FixServiceOptions)
Provide `createRuntimeDependencies` as the single entry point for command handlers to obtain all runtime prerequisites
Resolve execution profile (resolveExecutionProfileFromSettings) and command context (RuntimeCommand) for the current runtime request
Acquire and release a lock for the command to prevent concurrent runtime sessions.
Load execution checkpoint state and detect whether the session can resume from a previous incomplete run.
Warn the user if the session is resumable and if protected output mutations were detected.
Prepare the execution context via the provided `prepare` callback.
Execute the command with protected output write access wrapped.
Handle errors by recording a failed run in the checkpoint and invoking the `onError` callback.
Support runtime I/O abstraction via `RuntimeIO` for consistent console/log output.
Define the `RuntimeCommand` type union of 'sync' | 'check' | 'fix'.
Orchestrates the full sync workflow: file scanning, AST extraction, summarization, state commit, reconciliation, and post-commit derivation.
Manages protected output detection and warnings via spine-gate integration before committing changes.
Coordinates checkpointing and resume logic through ExecutionCheckpointStore to allow recovery from partial failures.
Manages language discovery and registry updates to ensure the sync operates on correct file types.
Evaluates repair policy decisions when the sync encounters drift or corruption.
Performs file hashing via Manifest to detect stale summaries needing regeneration.
Wraps the entire workflow inside a runtime session (runWithRuntimeSession) for transactional boundaries and error scoping.
Parse relevance diagnostics mode from the SPINE_DIAGNOSTICS_MODE environment variable.
Open the project manifest and create task state and task artifacts state.
Instantiate and configure Scanner, Aggregator, ASTExtractor, RuleEngine, OutputManager, ContextEngine, and LockManager.
Combine all initialized components into a single PreparedTaskContext object for downstream use.
Expose the ServiceOptions interface to define required inputs (rootDir, llmClient, scanPolicy).
Check the existence of key files (config, rules, manifest, index, view) in the .spine directory to determine repo posture.
Discover the language landscape: classify files by language using Scanner, Config, LangRegistry, and discoverLanguages, producing aggregated snapshots per language.
Generate AST previews for each discovered language by selecting up to 10 files per language, extracting AST via ASTExtractor, and returning preview results.
Generate a Markdown artifact that summarizes architectural diagnostics (cycle reports, dead code reports, hub reports) along with loaded rules.
Escape Markdown inline characters safely to prevent rendering issues.
Implement the ViewProducer interface, accepting a ViewContext and returning a ViewArtifact.
Load rules from the configured directory using loadRulesFromDir to include in the briefing output.
Reads the knowledge graph from a well-known JSON file under .spine/view/data/.
Derives architecture diagram data (SVG rendering and structured JSON) from the knowledge graph.
Saves the generated SVG and JSON artifacts via the output manager, adhering to view artifact contracts.
Implements the ViewProducer interface for the architecture diagram view type.
Load the knowledge graph from disk using fs/path.
Compute change impact (blast radius) for each module via the changeImpact engine function.
Filter out modules with zero impact and sort entries by totalAffected descending.
Render a Markdown report with a table showing total affected, depth breakdown, and rules affected for each module.
Save the view artifact and Markdown through ctx.outputManager.
Log progress information via ctx.runtimeIO.
Load indexed units from the .spine/index directory recursively using isCompatibleIndexDocument and readIndexDocument.
Cache the loaded units to avoid redundant filesystem I/O across view generation calls.
Sort folders by descending child count (and alphabetically) to prioritize folders with most structure for architecture diagram generation.
Limit the number of folders processed for architecture diagrams to MAX_ARCH_DIAGRAM_FOLDERS (12).
Report warnings via RuntimeIO when index documents fail compatibility checks or read errors occur.
Provide a global registry mapping view types (ViewType) to view producer functions (ViewProducer).
Allow registration of producers via registerViewProducer, typically called during module initialization for built-in views.
Provide lookup of a producer by view type via getViewProducer, returning undefined if unregistered.
Enumerate all registered view types via listViewTypes.
Define the ViewOutputManager abstraction for persisting view artifacts decoupled from concrete implementation.
Define the ViewContext type carrying runtime context (RuntimeIO, LLMClient, ViewIndexLoader) to view producers.
Define the ViewArtifact structure representing a single produced view artifact.
Define the ViewProducer interface that all view producers must implement, ensuring a uniform contract for producing views.
Re-export ViewType from the view types module for consumer convenience.
Load diagnostic JSON files from the .spine/view/data/ directory using fs and path.
Compute and format dependency edge compliance ratios into Markdown summary tables.
Format cycle dependency data (ID, modules, length) into a Markdown table with proper escaping.
Format dead code module entries into a Markdown table.
Provide escapeMarkdownTableCell utility to safely wrap values for Markdown table cells.
Classify each spine unit into a PublicSurfaceKind (function, class, CLI command, HTTP handler, etc.) using heuristics on the unit symbol data.
Score each candidate symbol based on documentation presence, naming convention compliance, re-export depth, and structural importance.
Filter out suppressed paths (via isSuppressedPath) to exclude noise like test files or temporary artifacts.
Sort and rank candidates by totalScore and remainingScore, capping the list to a manageable size.
Build and return a IViewArtifactEnvelope carrying IPublicSurfaceViewItem[], including metadata (schemaVersion, totalCount, score, confidence).
Analyzes loaded SpineUnit objects to identify code areas with elevated technical risk based on composite scoring.
Computes risk scores across multiple factors: rule violations, cross-boundary edges, adjacent test coverage, file age/churn, size/complexity, dependency fan-in, and suppression status.
Applies path suppression logic via isSuppressedPath to exclude non-actionable units.
Ranks and filters results to the top 12 risk hotspots, ordered by descending total score.
Produces structured IViewArtifactEnvelope payloads conforming to the view artifact schema, consumable by the view rendering pipeline.
Define the LoadedUnit interface that pairs a SpineUnit with its line count, representing a unit loaded from the index for further processing.
Maintain the canonical list of all system views (ViewDefinition) as an exported constant VIEW_DEFINITIONS.
Provide a type guard (isViewId) to validate whether a string is a known ViewId.
Expose a lookup function (getViewDefinition) that returns the ViewDefinition for a given ViewId.
Supply a function (getDefaultEnabledViewIds) that returns the subset of ViewIds marked as default enabled.
Offer a normalizer (normalizeViewIds) that splits a user-provided string array into known ViewIds and unrecognized strings, facilitating CLI argument parsing.
Load view Markdown templates from disk using Node.js fs and path modules.
Replace template placeholders with data from IViewArtifactEnvelope structures.
Format risk hotspot items into Markdown sections with entrypoint, kind, symbols, confidence, and summary.
Format public surface items into Markdown tables or lists.
Provide static methods (e.g., renderRiskHotspots) for use by view CLI commands and view runtime services.
Escape Markdown special characters in rendered output to preserve formatting integrity.
Resolve the boolean view layer state (enabled/disabled) from project config and env var SPINE_VIEW_LAYER, with fallback to default.
Resolve the set of enabled ViewIds by merging project-configured views, normalizing them, and identifying unknown IDs.
Track the source of each resolved value (project-config, env, or default) for transparency.
Starts an HTTP server on a user-configurable port (default 7899) for browsing generated view documentation.
Converts Markdown files from the .spine/pages directory to HTML using a subset Markdown-to-HTML converter embedded in the module.
Serves static files (HTML, SVG, JSON, etc.) from the root of the .spine directory.
Handles error responses with 404 (file not found) and 500 (internal errors).
Returns the http.Server instance to allow callers to gracefully stop the server.
Loads indexed source units via ViewIndexLoader using the configured root directory.
Builds ViewContext by combining indexed units with LLMClient and RuntimeIO for producer invocation.
Derives PublicSurfaceView artifacts by invoking buildPublicSurfaceView and writing results through OutputManager.
Derives RiskHotspotsView artifacts by invoking buildRiskHotspotsView and writing results through OutputManager.
Refreshes a given set of view IDs: clears stale artifacts, loads fresh units, runs registered producers, and writes new outputs.
Deletes all stored artifacts for specified view IDs via OutputManager.
Uses withProtectedOutputsWriteAccess and writeProtectedOutputBaseline to ensure safe output file writes.
Read and aggregate dashboard status from the .spine directory (existence, last sync timestamp, enabled view IDs, total rule count).
Encapsulate file I/O so CLI modules remain thin entrypoints without direct filesystem logic.
Expose a typed interface (DashboardData) describing the control plane's current state.
Detect mismatches between the intended artifact strategy and the current repository state (e.g., which agent instruction file is selected, whether managed blocks are present and complete).
Apply the desired artifact strategy by writing/updating config, gitignore, gitattributes, and agent instruction files.
Install default rule templates (layered-architecture.yml, naming-conventions.yml) into the repository's .spine/rules/ directory.
Synchronize V2 agent instruction blocks (health block, MCP tools block, briefing block, etc.) with the selected agent instruction file (e.g., CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md).
Synchronize all managed repository files (.gitignore, .gitattributes, .spineignore, .searchignore) with ArchSpine's managed blocks.
Inject npm scripts (e.g., `spine-sync`, `spine-scan`, `spine-check`) into the repository's package.json.
Return typed result structures detailing what was checked or applied.
Define hardcoded rule templates (e.g., 'no-core-to-cli') with filenames and YAML content.
Expose a list of all available rule templates as RuleTemplateEntry objects.
Add a specified rule template to a project directory by writing the template file to a predefined path.
Retrieve metadata (severity, title, summary) from a rule template's content by parsing its header lines.
Define the contract for a runtime service facade: getResolvedLLMSettings, getSyncService, getCheckService, getFixService.
Act as a placeholder to absorb future runtime orchestration responsibilities, preventing further expansion of provider-resolution code in infra/llm/runtime.ts.
Install the ArchSpine skill definition into Claude's skill directory under ~/.claude/skills/archspine/.
Write a state file to ~/.archspine/skill-state.json recording the skill installation.
Uninstall the skill by removing the skill file and state file, and cleaning up empty directories.
Ensure required directories exist before writing files.
Define visual styles (fill, stroke) for each architecture node type.
Layout nodes by a fixed layer order to produce consistent vertical grouping.
Calculate horizontal positions for nodes within each layer.
Escape all dynamic text to prevent SVG injection.
Generate a complete, self-contained SVG document string.
Converts a KnowledgeGraph (nodes and edges) into an SVG string suitable for architecture diagram visualization.
Implements a topological-sort based layered layout algorithm (Sugiyama-equivalent) to assign y-coordinates to layers and position nodes within layers.
Generates SVG elements for nodes (rectangles with labels) and edges (polylines with arrowheads), handling XML escaping and basic styling.
Provides the single exported function `generateArchitectureDiagramSvg` as the public API for downstream view services.
Filter out test, fixture, example, doc, dist, and build paths from analysis to avoid noise.
Aggregate individual contribution scores into a total sum for risk or health metrics.
Compute a normalized confidence value between 0 and 0.99 based on total score and number of supporting signals.
Extract the top-level directory boundary from a file path (first segment of normalized path).
Count how many incoming edges from other top-level boundaries a given SpineUnit receives, used for cross-boundary dependency analysis.
Resolve the target top-level boundary from a dependency path, handling internal and external references.
Provide a single, stable import point for all view subsystem functionality.
Expose architecture diagram rendering (both standard and SVG) via re-exported functions.
Expose architecture diagram view builder via re-exported symbols.
Expose shared utility functions and types from the common module.
Expose index document loader for reading .spine/index documents.
Expose public-surface view builder for generating public API surface documentation.
Expose risk-hotspots view builder for generating risk hotspot analysis.
Enforce boundary isolation so that internal view module changes do not affect external consumers.
invariants: The check pipeline always runs ScanAndCleanupTask, ASTExtractionTask, and ValidationTask in that order.
Task context is created via createTaskContext, which uses deriveCheckResumeCandidateFiles to enable resumable runs.
Errors are wrapped as ArchSpineError using toArchSpineError before propagation.
The runtime session is managed via runWithRuntimeSession, ensuring proper lifecycle hooks.
This service does not implement view-specific logic; it lives under src/services/ not src/services/view/.
Fix runs are always executed within a runtime session via runWithRuntimeSession.
The module enforces a maximum of 2 fix retry attempts (MAX_FIX_RETRIES).
All fix pipelines begin by scanning active violations from the project manifest.
All scanning, detection, and persistence logic is delegated to imported modules; this function only orchestrates.
The function must not perform I/O directly (except through the delegated modules).
The function must not contain any CLI-specific logic (e.g., console.log).
The function must not store state; it is a stateless service call.
Assumes Config and Secrets interfaces expose setLLMProvider, setLLMModel, setLLMBaseURL, setLLMApiKey, clearLLMApiKey methods.
Assumes getGlobalArchSpineDir returns the global ArchSpine directory.
LLMScope is strictly 'global' or 'project'.
The `.spine` directory must exist for a valid ArchSpine project.
A locked snapshot index (detected via lock file) blocks publishing.
Local artifact strategy should trigger a user-facing warning during publish preparation.
Must always produce a valid `ResolvedExecutionProfile` when given a fully resolved `ResolvedLLMSettings`.
The `generationStrategy` in the output must be a valid `GenerationStrategy` from the prompt-policy module.
The function must not have side effects (pure transformation).
All LLM client creation must pass through `createResolvedLLMClient` from `../infra/llm.js`
View resolution must use `resolveViewLayer` and `resolveEnabledViews` from `./view/index.js`
The service must never directly import view-specific modules from `../infra/` to avoid violating the service-runtime-boundary rule
The module must always import from infra modules (execution-checkpoint, spine-gate, runtime-io, writer-boundary) to fulfill its orchestration role.
The export `runWithRuntimeSession` is the only entry point for initiating a runtime session; all logic is encapsulated.
The sync workflow must be idempotent – re-running from scratch or resume produces consistent state.
All protected output mutations must be detected and confirmed by the user before being written.
Checkpointing must allow resume from the point of failure without redoing completed stages.
Language registry must be synchronized with the latest discovered languages before task execution.
The rootDir option must be a valid directory path.
The llmClient option must be provided if the context engine requires it.
The scanPolicy option must be provided to define scanning behavior.
The manifest must be openable and closeable.
[Rule: service-runtime-boundary] This service must not be placed under src/infra/; it correctly lives in src/services/.
All orchestration dependencies (Scanner, Config, discoverLanguages, LangRegistry, ASTExtractor) are sourced from engines, infra, or ast directories, consistent with the allowed boundary.
The produced artifact must be valid Markdown and safe for rendering (no unescaped special characters).
The output must handle missing or empty diagnostic data without crashing.
The rules directory must be readable and JSON well-formed; otherwise, fallback gracefully.
ViewContext must provide at least the diagnostic reports and rule configuration path.
The knowledge graph must exist at .spine/view/data/knowledge-graph.json; if absent, the producer should gracefully skip generation.
Output files are always named architecture-diagram.svg and architecture-diagram.json, placed relative to the knowledge graph path.
Implements the ViewProducer contract defined in ./producer.ts.
Impact calculation relies on the pre-computed knowledge graph under .spine/view/data/knowledge-graph.json.
Each module in the report must have an entry in the knowledge graph.
Depth values are capped at 3; if the impact cone exceeds depth 3, the entry is flagged as truncated.
Must only be used within the view service layer (src/services/view/).
Dependencies limited to fs, path, infra modules (index-reader, runtime-io), and protocol types.
Imported functions isCompatibleIndexDocument and readIndexDocument must come from ../../infra/index-reader.js.
The registry must be populated before any view execution request is processed.
Each view type can have at most one producer; re-registration overwrites the previous producer.
View producers must satisfy the ViewProducer interface, which includes a `produce` method returning ViewArtifact[].
ViewOutputManager.saveView must be called by producers to persist each artifact.
All view producer modules must reside under src/services/view/ to respect the service-runtime-boundary rule.
Input data must conform to the diagnostic JSON schema (KGNode, edges, cycles, deadCode).
Markdown output must be valid GitHub-flavored Markdown with properly escaped table cells.
The output artifact envelope must always set metadata.schemaVersion to CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION.
Every non-suppressed LoadedUnit must produce at most one ScoredCandidate entry.
Scoring computation must be deterministic for identical input units.
The totalScore at the envelope level is derived from aggregated subset of individual candidate scores.
Output is always limited to MAX_RISK_HOTSPOTS (12) items.
Scoring algorithm keys on seven discrete risk factors; no factor is added or removed without changing the analysis contract.
Path suppression via isSuppressedPath is applied before scoring, not after.
No function or class exists in this module; contract is purely structural.
All view definitions must be declared in the VIEW_DEFINITIONS array and must have unique ViewIds.
The VIEW_DEFINITION_MAP (derived from VIEW_DEFINITIONS) must contain every view referenced by lookup functions.
Functions isViewId, getViewDefinition, getDefaultEnabledViewIds, normalizeViewIds must remain pure and side-effect free.
View-specific rendering logic must reside in src/services/view/ (service-runtime-boundary rule).
All rendering methods must return a plain Markdown string, never HTML or other formats.
Template loading must use synchronous fs calls (as seen in the AST) to keep rendering simple and deterministic.
The module does not mutate the Config object; it only reads via defined accessors.
All resolved outputs are derived from inputs; no side effects on external state.
Normalization of view IDs is delegated to view-registry functions.
The server port must be configurable; default is 7899.
The server must serve files from the .spine directory specified in ViewServerOptions.
Markdown files must be converted to HTML before sending to clients.
The server must handle multiple concurrent requests.
The server must handle errors gracefully without crashing the process.
All view producers must be registered in the producer registry before refreshViews is called for a given view ID.
OutputManager must have write access to the configured output directory; writes are gated by withProtectedOutputsWriteAccess.
The root directory provided to the constructor must contain an ArchSpine index (generated by the indexer).
refreshViews first deletes existing artifacts for the view IDs, then re-derives and writes new ones.
PublicSurface and RiskHotspots views are produced using dedicated builder functions (buildPublicSurfaceView, buildRiskHotspotsView) rather than generic producers.
The module resides under src/services/, not src/infra/, satisfying the service-runtime-boundary rule's intent.
All filesystem operations target only the .spine directory relative to the provided rootDir.
No side-effects beyond reading the filesystem.
The artifact strategy (ArtifactStrategy) must be consistent between the config and the actual repository state after application.
All managed blocks must be present and contain exactly the expected markers (e.g., BEGIN/END comments).
The repository must have the required configuration files (package.json, .gitignore, .gitattributes) before strategy application can succeed.
All template names must be keys in the internal RULE_TEMPLATES record.
Adding a template always writes the template file to rootDir/<derived-subdirectory>/<filename> based on template content.
The getRuleTemplateInfo function assumes the template content follows a specific Markdown header format: first line '# title', second line '# summary', etc.
The interface is a draft; all methods return unknown, indicating lack of concrete types.
The file should not be imported or implemented until the draft is stabilized.
Skill and state directories are always under the user's home directory.
State file is JSON-serializable.
Every ArchNodeType must have a corresponding entry in NODE_STYLES (enforced by static Record).
Layer order is fixed as: frontend, backend, messagebus, database, security, cloud, external.
Output is always a valid SVG string with width/height viewBox and proper element nesting.
Module does not depend on infra modules (only imports view types).
File location respects service-runtime-boundary rule (src/services/view/).
Input must be a valid KnowledgeGraph object with nodes and edges arrays.
Output SVG must be a well-formed XML string that can be embedded in HTML or saved as .svg file.
Node positions are computed deterministically based on graph topology; the same graph always produces the same layout.
All exported functions are synchronous and pure (no side effects, no external state).
Dependencies are limited to built-in `path` module and project-internal type definitions (`SpineUnit`, `IViewScoreContribution`).
Suppressed path patterns are hard-coded as a regular expression; no runtime configuration alters this behavior.
Confidence calculation caps at 0.99 and floors at 0, using a fixed formula.
All view-specific service modules must reside under src/services/view/.
This barrel file must re-export only public view subsystem symbols.
Consumers must import from this index file rather than from individual view modules to maintain encapsulation.

publicSurface.description: One-shot check function accepting rootDir, optional LLMClient, and optional ScanPolicy. Creates a default runtime I/O, instantiates CheckService internally, and runs the check pipeline. Used by CLI or scripts that need minimal setup.

Programmatic orchestration class for the check pipeline. Accepts CheckServiceOptions for full control. Exposes a `run(context?)` method that executes the pipeline tasks in order. Consumed by the CLI command handler and programmatic consumers.
Configuration interface for CheckService. Includes rootDir (required), optional llmClient, optional scanPolicy, and optional runtimeIO for custom logging and file operations.
Standalone async function that executes a full fix pipeline given a root directory and optional LLM client and scan policy. Returns a FixRunSummary.
Class that encapsulates the fix orchestration logic with configurable options. Provides a method (presumably run) to execute the pipeline.
Interface describing the outcome of a fix run, including initial and remaining violation counts and number of fixes applied.
Interface defining configuration options for the FixService, such as root directory and optional LLM client.

Scans the project at rootDir using the configured scanner policy, detects languages from the collected files, and persists the result to the manifest. Called by CLI bootstrap during project initialization or refresh.
Determines the correct config store, secrets store, and label for the given LLM scope (global or project), returning an LLMCommandTarget.
Writes a single LLM configuration value (provider, model, base-url, or api-key) into the target's config or secrets store.
Removes the LLM API key from the target's secrets store and returns a confirmation message.
Persists provider, model, and API key to the target stores in one call for a complete LLM setup.
Constructs a view model of current LLM configuration by merging project and global settings, including resolved provider, model, base URL, and API key presence.
Interface defining the contract for config store, secrets store, and label required to persist LLM settings.
Interface representing a complete LLM setup with provider, optional model, and optional API key.
Interface representing the structured output of buildLLMStatusViewModel for display.
Type alias restricting LLM configuration scope to either 'global' or 'project'.
Validates that the `.spine` directory exists at the given root. Throws ArchSpineError with code `PublishRuntimeMissing` if missing.
Validates that the snapshot index exists and is not locked. Throws ArchSpineError if the lock payload indicates an active snapshot write.
Checks the configured artifact strategy and emits a warning via RuntimeIO when the strategy is 'local', advising the user of non-production publishing.
Interface defining an execution profile with a generation strategy and its source identifier. Used by runtime services to determine how to generate responses.
Union type ('sync' | 'check' | 'fix') representing the currently selected runtime command. Consumed by orchestrators to branch execution flow.
Pure function that maps a ResolvedLLMSettings object to a ResolvedExecutionProfile. Called by runtime initialization to derive the active generation strategy.

Central runtime orchestrator that initializes and composes infrastructure dependencies (Config, LLM client, Secrets, View layer) and provides factory methods to create SyncService, CheckService, and FixService instances. Used by CLI command handlers to bootstrap the execution context.

Executes a runtime session for a given command (sync, check, fix). Handles checkpoint loading, protected output mutation detection, lock acquisition, context preparation, command execution, and error recording. Returns the result of the execute callback.
Type union representing the three runtime commands: 'sync', 'check', 'fix'. Used by callers to specify which command is being run.
Primary orchestrator class that executes the entire ArchSpine sync workflow. Consumed by CLI commands and the MCP server to trigger a sync operation.
Data structure returned after a sync execution, summarizing counts of scanned, extracted, summarized, committed, and reconciled files.
Configuration options that control how a single sync run behaves, including resume mode, repair policy, and protected output handling.
Configuration options that determine which stages of the sync pipeline are executed (e.g., skip summarize, skip reconcile).

Factory function that accepts TaskContextOptions and returns a PreparedTaskContext. It initializes all engines and infra components, parses environment-based diagnostics mode, and wires everything together. This is the primary way to bootstrap a task execution.
Interface defining the configuration shape required to create a task context: rootDir, llmClient, scanPolicy, and other optional settings. Consumers must pass an object conforming to this interface.

Interface representing the fully initialized runtime context, exposing references to Scanner, Aggregator, ASTExtractor, RuleEngine, OutputManager, ContextEngine, LockManager, task state, etc. Consumed by the task execution pipeline.
Returns a RepoPosture object indicating the presence of key .spine files (config, rules, manifest, index, view) in the given root directory.
Scans the repository to classify files by programming language, producing a LanguagePreview map with file counts and total sizes per language.
For each language in a snapshot, selects non-test source files and returns AST previews (file path, size, and extracted AST) for up to 10 files per language.
Interface representing the boolean presence of canonical .spine configuration/metadata files.
Interface containing language name, file count, and total bytes for a discovered language.
Interface containing an array of language AST snapshots (LanguageASTSnapshot) and the set of languages with previews.

Exported default function that implements the ViewProducer interface. Accepts a ViewContext containing CycleReport, DeadCodeReport, HubReport, and a path to rules; returns a ViewArtifact with the Markdown brief. Called by the view orchestration engine to generate the briefing page.

Primary function that reads the knowledge graph, produces architecture diagram artifacts (SVG and JSON), and persists them via the output manager. Consumed by the view runtime to generate the architecture diagram view.
Exports a ViewProducer component that generates the change impact Markdown report for the ArchSpine view system. Consumed by view-registry to populate the change-impact view.
Service class that loads, caches, and serves indexed Spine units (folders/projects/source files) for view generation. Primary consumer interface for view renderers to access the index.
Constructs a ViewIndexLoader with the root directory path and optional RuntimeIO for warnings.
Returns the cached list of LoadedUnit objects, reloading from the filesystem if not yet cached. Sorts folders by child count.
Registers a ViewProducer function for a given ViewType. Called by each built-in view module to announce its producer during initialization.
Retrieves the registered ViewProducer for the specified ViewType, or undefined if no producer has been registered.
Returns an array of all ViewType strings for which a producer has been registered.
Re-exports the ViewType union from the view types module, allowing consumers to reference available view types without additional imports.
Interface defining the abstract persistence surface for view artifacts, decoupling producers from the concrete OutputManager implementation.
Interface providing runtime context (RuntimeIO, LLMClient, ViewIndexLoader) to view producers during the produce execution.
Interface representing a single produced artifact, containing metadata such as type, content, and file name for a view.
Interface that all view producers must implement; requires a `produce` method that takes ViewContext and returns an array of ViewArtifact.

Main entry point that consumes an array of LoadedUnit objects, filters and scores each unit into a ScoredCandidate, sorts by score, and returns an IViewArtifactEnvelope with IPublicSurfaceViewItem data. Called by the view runtime or view server to produce the public-surface artifact.

Analyzes an array of LoadedUnit objects and produces a structured IViewArtifactEnvelope containing up to 12 ranked risk hotspot items with composite scores, factor breakdowns, and confidence levels. Consumed by the view runtime for rendering the risk hotspots dashboard page.
Interface representing a loaded spine unit from the index, including the unit data and its line count for processing or display.
The canonical array of ViewDefinition objects, listing all system views. Consumed by the CLI, view-service, and view producers to enumerate and describe available views.
Type guard that checks whether a string matches a known ViewId. Used for runtime validation of user-provided view identifiers.
Returns the full ViewDefinition object for a given ViewId. Used by other modules that need view metadata (title, description, requirements).
Returns an array of ViewIds that have defaultEnabled set to true. Used by the CLI and view runtime to determine which views to run when none are explicitly selected.
Takes an optional array of strings and splits them into known ViewIds and unknown strings. Used by CLI argument parsing to gracefully handle invalid view names.
Interface specifying the structure of a view: its unique id, title, description, and flags for default enablement, full-sync requirement, and LLM requirement.
Static utility class with methods to render view artifacts (risk hotspots, public surface) into Markdown. Used by view CLI commands and the view runtime to produce human-readable output.
Interface representing the resolved boolean state of the view layer, including the source of resolution (project-config, env, or default).
Interface representing the resolved list of enabled ViewId values, their resolution source, and any unknown IDs that were not recognized.

Reads the view layer setting from project config (via getViewLayer) and env var SPINE_VIEW_LAYER, then returns a ResolvedViewLayer with source tracking. Called during system startup to decide whether the view system is active.

Reads the configured enabled views from project config, normalizes them using view-registry, falls back to defaults, and returns a ResolvedEnabledViews with source and unknown IDs. Called to determine which specific views are active.
Starts a lightweight HTTP server that serves generated view pages from the .spine directory; returns the http.Server instance for external shutdown control.
Configuration interface containing the path to the .spine directory (spineDir) and an optional port number (port, defaults to 7899).

Primary orchestrator for deriving, refreshing, and deleting ArchSpine view artifacts. External consumers instantiate this class with infrastructure dependencies (ViewIndexLoader, OutputManager, RuntimeIO, LLMClient) and call its methods to manage views.
Initializes the ViewService with the given root directory, a ViewIndexLoader for loading indexed units, and the necessary I/O and inference services.
Derives the PublicSurface view artifact from indexed source units in the root directory and writes it to the output directory via OutputManager.
Derives the RiskHotspots view artifact from indexed source units in the root directory and writes it to the output directory via OutputManager.
Given an array of ViewId, deletes existing artifacts for those views, reloads indexed units, invokes the appropriate view producers, and writes the updated artifacts.
Deletes all stored output artifacts associated with the specified view IDs.
Reads and returns dashboard status from the .spine control plane. Used by CLI commands to display current ArchSpine state.
Data transfer object describing the .spine control plane status: hasSpine, lastSync timestamp, list of enabledViews, and rulesCount.
Compares the current repository configuration against the intended artifact strategy and returns a list of issues to resolve. Called during `spine init` and `spine sync` preflight checks.

Applies the next artifact strategy to the repository, updating config, agent instruction files, and managed blocks accordingly. Called during `spine init` and `spine sync` to enforce the desired setup.
Copies the default architectural rule templates (layered-architecture.yml, naming-conventions.yml) into the .spine/rules directory of the repository. Used during first-time initialisation.
Synchronises V2 agent instruction blocks (health, MCP tools, briefing, etc.) into the selected agent instruction file. Called by syncAgentInstructionsConfiguration.
Full synchronisation of agent instruction configuration: updates health blocks, MCP tools blocks, V2 blocks, removes stale sections, and reports sync status. Called during `spine sync`.
Synchronises managed files (gitignore, gitattributes, spineignore, searchignore) with ArchSpine's managed blocks using the given artifact strategy. Called during `spine sync`.
Adds or updates npm scripts for ArchSpine commands (spine-sync, spine-scan, etc.) in the repository's package.json. Called during `spine init`.
Typed result of checkRepositoryStrategy, containing the current strategy, next strategy, and a list of identified issues.
Typed result of applyRepositoryStrategy, reporting which strategy was applied and whether files were updated.
Typed result of installRecommendedRuleTemplates, listing whether each template file was created or already existed.
Typed result of syncAgentInstructionsConfiguration, containing sync statuses for agent instructions, health blocks, MCP tools blocks, and V2 blocks.
Typed result of syncManagedRepositoryFiles, reporting sync status for each managed file (gitignore, gitattributes, spineignore, searchignore).
Typed result of injectRepositoryPackageScripts, detailing which scripts were added or updated.
Typed collection of V2 block sync statuses, used inside AgentInstructionsSyncResult to describe the outcome of syncV2AgentInstructionBlocks.
Returns an array of all available rule template entries, each containing the template name, filename, and parsed title. Used by consumers that need to enumerate or display rule options.

Adds a predefined rule template by name to the specified project root directory, writing the template file to the correct subpath. Used by scaffolding or configuration commands to populate rule definitions.
Parses and returns metadata (severity, title, summary) from a given rule template's content. Used by UI or CLI to display rule properties without loading external files.
Interface defining the shape of a rule template entry: name, filename, and title. Used as the return type of listRuleTemplates and as a data contract for consumers.

Interface defining the draft contract for a runtime service that provides resolved LLM settings and access to sync, check, and fix services. All methods currently return unknown, indicating the contract is not yet finalized.
Installs the ArchSpine skill definition to ~/.claude/skills/archspine/SKILL.md and creates a state file. Returns success or failure with a message.
Removes the installed skill file and state file, cleaning up empty directories. Returns removal status.

Class with a single static method 'render(spec: IArchDiagramSpec): string' that produces an SVG diagram string. Called by the diagram view producer to generate graphical output for architecture diagrams.
Takes a KnowledgeGraph and returns an SVG string representing a layered architecture diagram. This is the sole public API consumed by the view service layer to produce diagram views.
Returns true if the given file path matches known suppressed directories (tests, fixtures, examples, docs, dist, build). Used to exclude non-essential paths from analysis.
Sums an array of score contributions to produce a total score for a given view metric.
Converts a total score and support count into a normalized confidence value between 0 and 0.99 for display or threshold checks.
Extracts the top-level directory segment from a normalized file path, used for boundary detection.
Counts how many incoming dependencies cross top-level boundaries for a given SpineUnit, used to identify architectural coupling risks.
Renders the architecture diagram in text format. Exported from arch-diagram-renderer.
Renders the architecture diagram as SVG. Exported from arch-diagram-svg-renderer.
Builds the architecture diagram view data structure. Exported from architecture-diagram-view.
Loads and parses index documents from the .spine/index directory. Exported from index-loader.
Builds the public surface view data from scanned symbols. Exported from public-surface-view.
Builds the risk hotspots view data from analysis results. Exported from risk-hotspots-view.

Parse and validate the `ValidatePolicy` configuration value (accepted: 'default', 'strict'); return `undefined` for unrecognized values.
Parse and validate the `RelevanceDiagnosticsMode` configuration value; return `undefined` for unrecognized values.
Provide case-insensitive, safe parsing that never throws.
Define default validate policy mapping from PromptTaskMode to ValidatePolicy (strict for 'validate' mode, default otherwise).
Resolve validate policy by accepting an optional explicit policy, falling back to the default computed from task mode.
Expose a stable, reusable function for policy determination used by upstream prompt infrastructure.
Trim a string to a maximum number of lines, appending truncation indicator.
Trim a string to a maximum number of characters, preserving whole lines up to the limit.
Split concatenated rule data into individual rule blocks using a delimiter pattern.
Trim a concatenated rule string to a character limit, keeping complete rule blocks.
Trim sectioned context (separated by double newlines) to a character limit, preserving whole sections.
Compact a FileSkeleton object into a budget-aware string representation for prompt inclusion.
Trim the previous semantic context object to a maximum number of responsibility entries.

Provide a single stable import surface that isolates consumers from internal prompt submodule restructuring.

Re-export generateDocumentPrompt to produce prompts for atlas documentation units.

Re-export generateProjectPrompt to produce prompts for overall project context.

Re-export generateSourcePrompt to produce prompts for individual source file analysis.
Evaluate a ProtectedOutputMutationReport and produce a RepairPolicyDecision.
Classify each changed/added/removed path as a source file, aggregate-level path, or unmapped path.
Determine if aggregate-level paths are sufficiently covered by targeted source files.
Compute the appropriate repair action (targeted-repair, prompt-full-rebuild, require-full-rebuild).
Provide a safe non-interactive downgrade option when conditions allow.
Read the content of a managed block (delimited by start/end markers) from a given file path, returning null if the file does not exist.
Check whether a managed block string (or null) contains all required string entries via an every+includes test.
List all snapshot output files tracked by git under a root directory using `git ls-files` with pattern matching.
Check whether the standard snapshot output files (data/ and pages/ subdirectories under .spine) exist on disk.
Initializes a credential store using the provided root directory and optional backend.
Provides synchronous methods to get, set, clear, and check existence of secrets.
Exposes metadata about the credential source and backend name for diagnostics.
Encapsulates the credential store implementation behind a stable, project-level API.
Detect additions, changes, or deletions of files in the protected .spine/index and .spine/view output directories relative to a stored baseline.

Format a human-readable warning string summarizing detected mutations for CLI or other consumers.
Wraps asynchronous actions to fold their console output into a single line with a blinking indicator.
Provides fallback behavior for non-TTY or CI environments to avoid ANSI escape issues.
Allows user to press SPACE to expand and see the full log output in real-time.
Define the set of protected directories (e.g., .spine/index, .spine/view) that should remain read-only under normal operation.

Maintain invariants that protected directories are never writable outside of explicit write-access calls.
